name: USD Debasement & Hard Asset Premium
description: The structural expansion of the Fed balance sheet, record US deficit spending (>6% GDP), and de-dollarization pressures from BRICS+ create a long-term tailwind for hard assets — gold, silver, commodities, and energy — as dollar purchasing power erodes. Central bank gold accumulation (1,000+ tonnes/year) confirms the institutional thesis.
bear_case: A debt-deflation shock or aggressive Fed hawkishness could strengthen the dollar sharply, crushing gold and commodities. If the US government unexpectedly achieves fiscal consolidation (unlikely but possible post-election), the deficit narrative unwinds. Dollar dominance has proven more durable than bears expected repeatedly in the past — the Eurodollar system has structural USD demand that offsets supply expansion.

second_order_effects.description: Gold reprices toward $3,000–3,500 as institutional allocations shift from 0.5% to 2%+ of portfolios
Commodity-exporting emerging markets (Brazil, Australia, Canada) outperform as dollar weakens
Energy sector real returns expand as oil reprices in weak-dollar environment
Mining equity premium expands as CapEx underspend meets rising commodity prices
Real asset REITs (infrastructure, timber, farmland) attract rotation from financial assets
Bitcoin and crypto attract safe-haven bid as digital gold narrative strengthens

assumptions.text: US fiscal deficit remains >4% of GDP through the cycle
Fed cannot credibly commit to long-term balance sheet normalization
Central bank gold demand continues at 800+ tonnes/year
De-dollarization in trade settlement accelerates meaningfully
Real interest rates stay negative or near zero over the thesis horizon

invalidation_conditions.description: DXY Dollar Index breaks above 115 and holds for 2+ months on fiscal discipline signals
US Congress passes credible multi-year deficit reduction plan (>$2T over 10 years)
Gold drops below $1,800 — signals market disbelief in the debasement thesis
Fed embarks on aggressive QT that actually shrinks balance sheet below $7T

catalysts.description: Any dovish pivot or pause signals dollar weakness and gold bullish.
Large issuance volumes confirm deficit spending trajectory.
Any announcement of commodity-backed trade currency accelerates thesis.

bets.name: Long Gold via GLD ETF
bets.notes: Core hard asset position. Sized to 8% of portfolio.

scenarios.notes: Dollar collapses on debt ceiling crisis or new QE
Dollar squeeze from global risk-off, gold sold for liquidity

bets.name: Long Gold Miners via GDX
bets.notes: Leveraged play on gold with operational leverage. Higher vol than GLD.

scenarios.notes: Gold drops, energy costs rise — miner squeeze

bets.name: Long Copper Miners via COPX
bets.notes: Copper = real economy debasement hedge + AI infrastructure demand overlap.

conviction_entries.note: Initiating thesis. Fed balance sheet at $7.7T, deficit widening. Core setup in place.
Gold breaks $2,100 ATH. Central bank buying confirmed at 1,037 tonnes in 2023. Conviction rising.
Dollar index holding 104 but gold still elevated — suggests paper dollar demand decoupling from gold.
Gold $2,650. Fed cutting. China and India continuing aggressive gold accumulation. All assumptions holding.
Trump tariffs create dollar strength headwind short-term. Watching DXY closely. Still long-term bullish.

name: AI Infrastructure Supercycle
description: The buildout of AI training and inference capacity represents the largest CapEx cycle since the 1990s internet boom. Hyperscalers are committing $300B+ annually in data center spend through 2026. This creates compounding demand across semiconductors, power infrastructure, cooling systems, and copper interconnects — with supply constrained in all categories.
bear_case: AI capex could prove cyclical, not structural — if model performance plateaus (hitting a scaling wall), hyperscalers cut spending sharply. Regulatory intervention on AI could create multi-year headwinds. China semiconductor advancement could commoditize AI chips faster than expected, collapsing NVDA's pricing power. OpenAI's DeepSeek-style efficiency gains reduce compute requirements dramatically.

second_order_effects.description: Power grid demand surge — data centers require 50GW+ of new capacity by 2030, straining US grid
Semiconductor shortage 2.0 — TSMC advanced node capacity constrained through 2026
Data center real estate premium — specialized REITs (EQIX, DLR) see pricing power
Nuclear energy revival — tech companies sign offtake deals for reliable 24/7 carbon-free power
Copper demand explosion — AI data centers use 4x the copper of traditional buildings
Cooling technology boom — liquid cooling companies (VRT, Vertiv) see structural demand
Grid modernization CapEx — utilities and industrials exposed to grid upgrade cycle

assumptions.text: Hyperscaler AI capex sustains at >$200B/year through 2026
NVDA maintains 70%+ AI chip market share for 3+ years
Power grid constraints remain a binding bottleneck through 2027
Enterprise AI adoption accelerates, justifying inference compute expansion
No major regulatory crackdown on AI development in US/EU

invalidation_conditions.description: NVDA revenue misses by >15% for two consecutive quarters — signals AI capex pullback
Microsoft, Google, or Amazon publicly cuts AI capex guidance by >20%
Open-source AI model achieves GPT-4 quality at 1/10th compute cost — destroys NVDA moat
Semiconductor index (SMH) breaks below 2023 highs and holds for 3+ months

catalysts.description: Blackwell GPU demand confirmation. Any data center revenue beat is thesis-accelerating.
Azure AI capacity announcements. Watch for capex guidance.
Potential export controls on AI chips. Risk event for thesis.
TPU capacity and Gemini deployment signals.

bets.name: Long Semiconductors via SMH
bets.notes: Diversified semiconductor exposure. Less single-stock NVDA risk.

scenarios.notes: AI capex accelerates, TSMC expands, all semis rerate
Steady capex, moderate growth, semiconductors consolidate gains
AI capex cycle pauses, inventory correction

bets.name: Long Nuclear/Power via VST
bets.notes: AI power demand play. Vistra has nuclear + nat gas fleet perfectly positioned.

scenarios.notes: Tech offtake deals, power shortage premium
Gradual re-rate as power scarcity becomes consensus
Regulatory rollback of nuclear, gas prices spike costs

bets.name: Long NVDA (Speculative)
bets.notes: High conviction single-stock. Blackwell supercycle. Small size vs SMH due to concentration risk.

scenarios.notes: Blackwell fully sold out, data center revenue doubles
Steady growth, P/E compression offsets revenue growth
Competition from AMD/Intel, China restrictions bite

conviction_entries.note: GPT-4 moment clear. Hyperscaler capex guidance blowing out. Initiating with high conviction.
NVDA Q3 earnings: revenue $18.1B vs $16B est. Data center segment +400% YoY. Thesis confirmed.
NVDA Q1 FY25: $26B revenue. Blackwell transition ahead of schedule. Power grid strain becoming mainstream narrative.
DeepSeek concerns surface — open source efficiency improving faster than expected. Monitoring NVDA moat carefully.
DeepSeek R1 launches: inference efficiency 10x better. Short-term concern for demand. Long-term Jevons paradox may apply — lower cost = more usage.

name: Yield Curve Re-steepening
description: The yield curve inverted sharply in 2022-2023 as the Fed hiked aggressively. As the Fed cuts short rates while long-end inflation expectations remain elevated (fiscal dominance, structural commodity demand), the curve re-steepens. This creates a structural tailwind for bank net interest margins (borrow short, lend long) and pressure on long-duration bond holders. The 10Y-2Y spread normalizing to +50-150bps is the base case.
bear_case: The curve re-steepens via a bear-steepener (long rates rise on inflation fears, growth collapses) rather than a bull-steepener (short rates fall, long rates stable). This could tank both equities and regional banks simultaneously. Alternatively, a deflation shock (hard landing) causes the curve to steepen via collapsing short rates but also crushes loan demand, negating the NIM benefit for banks.

second_order_effects.description: Bank NIM (net interest margin) expands as borrow-short-lend-long dynamics return to profitability
Pension fund liability-duration mismatch improves, reducing forced long-bond buying
Dollar funding pressures ease as short-term USD rates decline
Regional bank outperformance as community lenders see NIM expansion first
Insurance sector benefits — reinvestment yields improve
EM sovereign debt stress in high-USD-debt countries eases as dollar weakens

assumptions.text: Fed cuts short-term rates 150-200bps through 2025 as inflation moderates
10Y yield stays above 4% due to term premium and fiscal concerns
No recession severe enough to cause a panic flight into long bonds
Bank credit quality remains manageable (CRE losses contained)

invalidation_conditions.description: 10Y-2Y spread exceeds +200bps via bear-steepening (growth collapse signal)
Fed pauses cuts before reaching 3.5% — inflation re-acceleration kills the thesis
Major US bank failure triggers systemic credit event
KRE (regional bank ETF) drops below $40 and holds — market voting against NIM thesis

catalysts.description: 25bps cut = thesis on track. Pause = conviction reduces.
Inflation print above 3.5% would pause Fed cuts, challenging the thesis.
Watch NIM guidance from JPM, BAC, WFC — confirmation or denial of the core bet.
Weak demand (high tail) signals long-end concerns, could cause bear steepener.

bets.name: Long Regional Banks via KRE
bets.notes: Direct NIM expansion play. KRE has highest NIM sensitivity of any ETF.

scenarios.notes: Bull steepener: Fed cuts 200bps, 10Y holds 4.2%, NIMs explode
Gradual NIM expansion, regional banks re-rate to 1.1x book
CRE losses mount, credit quality issues overshadow NIM gains

bets.name: Short Long Bonds via TBF
bets.notes: TBF is ProShares Short 20+ Year Treasury. Bet on 10Y staying elevated vs front end.

conviction_entries.note: Fed starting to signal cuts. Curve still deeply inverted at -50bps. Setting up the trade.
Fed cuts 50bps. 10Y-2Y spread moves from -50 to -20bps. Steepening begins.
Curve now +10bps. KRE up 18% since June. But Fed signaling slower cuts ahead — watching.
Strong jobs data pushing back Fed cut timeline. Long-end rising faster than expected. Risk of bear steepener increasing.
